qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: HTJ2K decode uses signed int row-byte arithmetic that overflows on very wide images
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an HTJ2K row-byte signed-overflow in `internal_ht.cpp`, while the advisory fixes a different root cause: PXR24 decompression accepts short zlib output in `compression.c` and then `undo_pxr24_impl` reconstructs pixels past `outSize` in `internal_pxr24.c`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding in this run identifies the advisory's PXR24 short-output handling and outSize-check omission; the only final qualifying finding is a separate HTJ2K arithmetic-overflow bug.
evidence: The input manifest lists exactly one final qualifying finding: `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_8fe894f5/htj2k_decode_stride_overflow`.
That finding report cites `src/lib/OpenEXRCore/compression.c:460` and `src/lib/OpenEXRCore/internal_ht.cpp:211` and `:216`, and its PoC verifies a UBSan signed-overflow in the HTJ2K decode path.

The advisory and upstream fix patch target `src/lib/OpenEXRCore/compression.c` short-output handling together with `src/lib/OpenEXRCore/internal_pxr24.c` bounds checks based on `outSize`, which is a different parser path and root cause.
The advisory impact is heap information disclosure from reading uninitialized PXR24 scratch bytes into decoded pixels, not the HTJ2K stride-overflow condition reported by the finding.
